opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

capacity.description: The storage capacity of the drive in GB (e.g., 4000 GB)

storage_type.description: The type of storage drive (either HDD or SSD)

form_factor.description: The physical size and shape of the drive (e.g., M.2-2280, 2.5", 3.5")

interface.description: The interface used by the drive (e.g., M.2 PCIe 4.0 X4, SATA 6Gb/s)

cache.description: The cache size of the drive in MB.

nvme.description: Whether the drive uses the NVMe protocol. Only applicable to SSDs

lighting.description: Lighting capabilities for this product. Use RGB for non-addressable RGB, ARGB for addressable RGB, Multicolor for non-RGB multi-color lighting, Fixed for a single fixed lighting color, None when the product has no lighting, Display for configurable displays, and Fixed Color Display for displays limited to fixed colors such as black and white.

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description: Product series name The manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the drive. Manufacturer, `capacity`, `form_factor`, `interface`, `storage_type` (SSD vs HDD), and `nvme` are all stored separately and MUST NOT be repeated in the series. Capacity is NEVER part of the series. Examples: 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB M.2-2280' → series='990 Pro'. 'WD_BLACK SN850X 2TB' → series='WD_BLACK SN850X' (the color tier is part of WD's marketed family name and IS part of the series for WD). 'Crucial MX500 1TB 2.5" SATA' → series='MX500'. The series stays consistent across capacity tiers of the same product line.

variant.description: Specific variant in the series Most drives should have an empty variant — capacity, form factor, interface, NVMe, and storage type are all stored in their own fields. The most common non-empty case is a heatsink-bundled SKU vs the bare-drive SKU of the same series + capacity. Examples: 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB' (bare) → variant=''. 'Samsung 990 Pro 2TB with Heatsink' → variant='Heatsink'. 'Seagate FireCuda 530 1TB Heatsink (PS5)' → variant='Heatsink'. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, capacity, form_factor, interface) group; if every drive collapses to the same value, leave it empty.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
